mission.title: kuberay: [Feature] Support managedBy for integration with MultiKueue
mission.description: [Feature] Support managedBy for integration with MultiKueue. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current kuberay deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kuberay version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuberay -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuberay
helm list -n kuberay 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kuberay installation.

steps.title: Review kuberay configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kuberay configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kuberay -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuberay
kubectl get configmap -n kuberay -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kuberay
```
The support for the managedBy field which can delegate reconciliation from built-in controller, to a custom one.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Support managedBy for integration with MultiKueue
steps.description:
## Why are these changes needed?
As stated in https://github.com/ray-project/kuberay/issues/2544.
It allows for integration with [MultiKueue](https://kueue.sigs.k8s.io/docs/tasks/manage/setup_multikueue/) (multi-cluster Kueue), in specific it provides:
* simpler installation, otherwise only installation of RayJob CRDs is required
* support for mixed setup in one cluster - some RayJobs could be run by MultiKueue and some by the default operator

## Related issue number
Relates to #2544 

##

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuberay -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuberay
kubectl get events -n kuberay --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Support managedBy for integration with MultiKueue" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Why are these changes needed?
As stated in https://github.com/ray-project/kuberay/issues/2544.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kuberay installed or the issue environment reproducible.
